summary: DHH highlights the launch of Twinkler by The Robot Co-op, a goal-tracking web app based on the idea that writing down your goals increases the likelihood of achieving them. The post briefly describes the concept and encourages readers to create their own lists. DHH also notes that Twinkler and the broader 43 Things platform are built on Ruby on Rails, showcasing early real-world adoption of the framework.
key_points: The Robot Co-op, a seven-person Seattle startup, launched Twinkler as a goal-setting and accountability tool
The premise is that writing down goals increases the chances of completing them
Twinkler and the full 43 Things site were built using Ruby on Rails, serving as an early showcase for the framework
money_quotes: 43 days until the New Year — What do you want to do with your life?
It's a well known fact that by writing down your goals you greatly increase the chances of actually completing them.
Twinkler is built using Ruby on Rails. So is the 'real' site.
key_insight: An early example of DHH promoting real-world Rails adoption by spotlighting startups building production applications with the framework.
